label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. So, the task is to determine which of the four options is true according to the passage provided. Let me read through the passage again carefully to make sure I understand all the details. First, the main topic is the improvement in water supply in southern Beijing, specifically regarding recycled water. The article mentions that the government has upgraded three recycled water plants, increasing capacity by 160,000 cubic meters per day. They're also building more sewage treatment plants to recycle sewage. An expert, Zhang Xiang, points out that using recycled water is cheaper than transferring water over long distances. The recycled water is mainly used for industry, landscaping, and cleaning. There's also mention of replacing coal-burning stoves with electric radiators in a three-year plan from 2013 to 2015. Now, looking at the options: A) Recycled water will be mainly used for drink. The passage says the recycled water is mainly used for industry, landscaping, and cleaning. So A is incorrect. B) As usual, recycled water is accepted by people. The passage mentions that in the past, people weren't enthusiastic about using recycled water, but now they're gradually accepting it as the quality has improved and more plants have been set up. So B is incorrect because it's not "as usual" (as in before), but rather a change from past behavior. C) Traditionally people in southern Beijing use coal-burning stove for cooking. The article states that the government will replace traditional coal-burning stoves with electric radiators as part of a plan. This implies that before, they were using coal-burning stoves. So C
